function_signatures.name: index_entry_in_the_penetration_testing_category_use_as_a_candidate_tool_in_the_chain_planner_after_a_nvd_lookup_pass_on_the_target_vendor
function_signatures.description: Index entry in the Penetration Testing category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.

function_signatures.name: pairs_with_the_penetration_testing_system_prompt_stanza_in_core_ai_backend_chain_to_teach_the_llm_the_standard_invocation_pattern
function_signatures.description: Pairs with the Penetration Testing system-prompt stanza in core.ai_backend.chain to teach the LLM the standard invocation pattern.

function_signatures.name: operator_initiated_the_chain_step_is_gated_by_the_per_step_accept_cancel_gate_the_tool_does_not_auto_execute
function_signatures.description: Operator-initiated: the chain step is gated by the per-step ACCEPT/CANCEL gate; the tool does not auto-execute.

function_signatures.description: Use as a teaching example in the prompt stanza so the LLM can reason about the right run order.

function_signatures.name: main
function_signatures.description: Top-level entry point for SharpHound (unknown/SharpHound). Reads CLI args, dispatches to subcommands.

function_signatures.name: run
function_signatures.description: Top-level CLI runner for SharpHound. Returns a result envelope (ok, data, error).

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd toolboxes/SharpHound && python3 SharpHound.py --help`. Further invocations from upstream: `cd toolboxes/SharpHound && cat README.md`; `cd toolboxes/SharpHound && ls -la`. ![GitHub all releases](https://img.shields.io/github/downloads/SpecterOps/SharpHound/total).
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [shell_linux] in the Penetration Testing category. Fits when the engagement needs: ![GitHub all releases](https://img.shields.io/github/downloads/SpecterOps/SharpHound/total).

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with unknown/CrackMapExec (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with winsiderss/systeminformer (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with v1lleneuve/sys-shred (score=10); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['shell_linux', 'shell_windows', 'web'] and phase_hint='exploit'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['shell_linux', 'shell_windows', 'web'] and phase_hint='exploit'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 5: same attack_surface=['shell_linux', 'shell_windows', 'web'] and phase_hint='exploit'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: Use during the active exploitation phase once recon has identified a service or surface on the engagement host.
Use as a fallback when the primary exploit path (CVE match, KB hit) did not yield a shell.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Pair with the LLM system-prompt stanza to ensure the chain planner knows how to call this tool.
